editorial.pullQuote: Korea's latest failure to secure a place on the watch list is undeniably disappointing.
summary: Korea failed again to make MSCI's Developed Markets Index watch list.
The setback exposes structural weaknesses in market accessibility and governance.

editorial.title: 정청래 당대표 연임 도전, 자신의 공과 냉철하게 돌아보길
editorial.body: (사설) 정청래 더불어민주당 대표가 사퇴하고 8월 전당대회 당대표 선거 출마를 예고했다. 이재명 정부 2년 차, 여당 내 갈등과 개혁 드라이브, 당청 불협화음 등 정 대표의 지난 10개월간 리더십이 도마에 올랐다. |
summary: 정청래 민주당 대표가 사퇴 후 8월 전당대회 출마를 예고했다.
지난 10개월간 당내 갈등, 개혁 드라이브, 당청 불협화음 등으로 리더십이 도마에 올랐다.
자신의 공과를 냉철하게 평가하고 돌아볼 필요가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 정 대표의 지난 10개월간 리더십이 도마에 올랐다.
editorial.stance: 당 대표는 당내 갈등과 리더십 논란에 대해 자기 성찰적 태도를 가져야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Jung Chung-rae Seeks Reelection as Party Chief, Eyes Candid Assessment of Record
summaryTr: Democratic Party Leader Jung Chul-rae announced his candidacy for the August national convention after his resignation.
Over the past 10 months, his leadership has faced scrutiny due to internal party conflict, reform initiatives, and discord between the party and the government.
He needs to coldly evaluate and reflect on his accomplishments and shortcomings.
editorial.bodyTr: Democratic Party Chairman Jung Chul-rae has announced his resignation and indicated his intention to run in the August party convention leadership election. As the Lee Jae-myung administration enters its second year, Chairman Jung's leadership over the past ten months has come under scrutiny amid internal party conflicts, reform initiatives, and discord between the party and the government.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: 사설이 정청래 당대표를 직접 비판하지도 옹호하지도 않고, 당내 갈등의 객관적 사실만 제시한 후 '자기 성찰'을 권고하는 중립적 톤으로 양쪽 입장 모두를 포용하는 균형잡힌 논조를 유지하고 있습니다.

editorial.titleTr: Editorial: 'Semiconductor Politics' Sparks Democratic Primary Clash, Widening Regional Divides from South to North Jeolla
summaryTr: Samsung and SK Hynix push ahead with plans to build semiconductor factories worth hundreds of trillions of won in Gwangju and South Jeolla Province
Lee Jae-myung emphasizes the necessity of diversifying strategic industries to break away from overconcentration in the Seoul metropolitan area
Semiconductor politics sparks regional conflict among Yeongnam, Chungcheong, South Jeolla, and North Jeolla provinces
editorial.bodyTr: The Democratic primary has triggered semiconductor politics, with conflict between Yeongnam and Chungcheong regions, and further tensions between South and North Jeolla provinces. Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are in discussions with the government on plans to build semiconductor fabrication lines worth hundreds of trillions of won in Gwangju and South Jeolla Province. President Lee Jae-myung also stated on the 25th that diversification of strategic industries across multiple poles is essential to overcome the Seoul metropolitan area's monopoly on economic power.

editorials.body: 보완수사권 완전 폐지, 정치 제물 된 한국의 형사사법 정부가 검사의 보완수사권을 예외 없이 완전 폐지하기로 형사소송법 개정 방침을 정했다고 한다. 보완수사권 완전 폐지는 민주당 강경파의 주장이었다. 다른 입장이던 정부가 이 주장을 수용하겠다는 것이다. 한국 형사

summary: 정부가 검사의 보완수사권을 완전 폐지하기로 결정했다.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Complete Abolition of Prosecutorial Supplementary Investigation Rights: Korea's Criminal Justice System Becomes Political Sacrifice
summaryTr: The government has decided to completely abolish prosecutors' supplementary investigation authority.
This is an amendment to the Criminal Procedure Act that accepts the arguments of hardline Democratic Party members.
This is criticism that Korean criminal justice is becoming a sacrifice to politics.
editorials.bodyTr: The government has decided to completely abolish prosecutors' supplementary investigative authority without exception through amendments to the Criminal Procedure Act. The complete abolition of supplementary investigative authority had been a demand of hardline Democratic Party members. The government, which previously held a different position, is now accepting this demand. Korea's criminal justice system has become a political sacrifice.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative

editorials.title: [사설] 도발한 北, 막아낸 美가 한마디도 등장 않는 6·25 기념사
editorials.body: 도발한 北, 막아낸 美가 한마디도 등장 않는 6·25 기념사 이재명 대통령이 25일 6·25 전쟁 76년을 맞아 기념사를 했다. A4 용지 2장 정도를 메울 만한 분량이었다. 대통령은 6·25 전쟁을 대한민국 역사상 가장 처절한 비극이었다고 규정했다. 그러나 그
editorials.pullQuote: 6·25 전쟁을 대한민국 역사상 가장 처절한 비극이었다고 규정했다.
summary: 이재명 대통령이 6·25 76년 기념사에서 전쟁을 처절한 비극으로 규정했다.
북한의 도발과 미국의 역할이 기념사에서 전혀 언급되지 않았다.
A4 2장 분량의 짧은 기념사로 논란이 예상된다.
editorials.stance: 도발국과 방어국에 대한 균형있는 역사 인식이 부족한 기념사 논평
editorials.titleTr: Editorial: North's Provocation, America's Response Absent from Korean War Anniversary Address
summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung characterized the war as a tragic catastrophe in his address commemorating the 76th anniversary of the Korean War.
North Korea's provocations and America's role were not mentioned at all in the commemorative address.
The brief commemorative address, spanning just two pages of A4 paper, is expected to spark controversy.
editorials.bodyTr: President Lee Jae-myung delivered a commemorative address on the 76th anniversary of the Korean War on the 25th. The speech was roughly two pages of A4 paper in length. The president characterized the Korean War as the most tragic event in South Korean history. However, he...

editorial.title: [사설] 감시 무풍지대 선관위의 끝 모를 도덕적 해이
editorial.body: 입력 2026.06.18 10:33 2026.06.18 09:43 2026.06.18 06:09 노태악 당시 중앙선거관리위원장이 지난 5일 ‘투표용지 부족 사태’ 관련 대국민 사과를 하고 있다. 뉴스1 중앙선거관리위원회가 투표용지 부족 사태를 초래한 6·3 지방선거 당시 일부 투표소에서 극심한 혼란상이 나타났던 것으로 확인되고 있다. 선거 당일 상황을 기록한 투표록에 따르면, 서울 잠실2동 한 투표소에선 투표관리관 도장이 찍히지 않은 채 용지가 배부됐다. 다른 투표소에선 바닥에 투표용지가 떨어져 있거나, 용지 부족 때문에 선관위에 전화를 걸어도 연락이 닿지 않았다고 한다. 추가 투표용지가 도착한 뒤에도 대기표를 받았던 주민들이 돌아오지 않아 참정권 행사가 불발됐다. 지난해 대선 때도 다른 사람의 선거인명부에 서명하고 투표한 사례가 2000건이 넘는 등 선거 관리 전반의 부실이 심각하다. 다 이유가 있었다. 선거로 업무가 바빠지는 해에 휴직자가 오히려 늘어나는 게 이번뿐 아니라 선관위의 전통이라고 한다. 이 조직이 공정한 선거 관리를 맡을 자격이 있는지 의문을 갖게 한다. 문제는 부실에서 그치지 않는다. 선관위 지도부부터 심각한 도덕적 해이에 빠져 있었다는 사실이 속속 드러나고 있다. 노태악 전 중앙선관위원장은 지난해 덴마크·스웨덴을 비롯해 재임 중 세 차례 간 해외 출장에 모두 배우자를 동반했다. 1200만원이 넘는 비즈니스 항공권 등의 경비를 선관위가 지불했다. 공식적인 외교나 국가 중대사도 아닌 해외 선거 관련 방문에 세금을 흥청망청 쓴 것이어서 외유성 출장이라는 비판을 면키 어렵다. 외부에 공개되는 출장 보고서에 ‘부부 동반’ 문구를 넣지 않은 것만 봐도 명백한 특혜가 아닐 수 없다. 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다. 선관위 직원들은 몰디브 등 세계적인 휴양지로 출장을 가고 선거 홍보물로 활용된다며 해변 사진을 버젓이 보고서에 첨부했다. 역량 강화를 이유로 이탈리아 등 관광지에도 매년 직원을 보냈다. 심지어 선관위 고위직들의 ‘자녀 특혜 채용’ 의혹이 불거진 시기에도 외유성 출장이 계속될 정도로 내부 기강 해이가 심각하다. 독립성을 방패 삼아 외부의 감시와 견제를 피해 성역을 구축해 온 선관위는 전면적인 개혁 없이는 국민의 신뢰를 회복할 수 없는 지경에 이르렀다. 예산 집행의 투명성 확보와 함께 채용 비리를 차단할 감시 시스템이 도입돼야 한다. 신뢰를 잃은 선관위가 관리하는 선거는 결과에 대한 불신의 씨앗을 남길 우려가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다.
summary: 6·3 지방선거에서 투표용지 부족으로 극심한 혼란 발생, 선관위 관리 부실 심각
노태악 전 위원장이 배우자 동반 해외출장에 1200만원대 세금 낭비
선관위는 독립성을 방패 삼아 감시를 피해온 후 전면 개혁 없이는 신뢰 회복 불가
editorial.stance: 선관위의 독립성 뒤에 숨은 적폐를 척결하고 예산·채용 감시 시스템을 도입해 민주주의의 기초인 선거 신뢰를 회복해야 한다
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: The Election Commission's Bottomless Moral Hazard in the Surveillance Gray Zone
summaryTr: Severe chaos erupted during the June 3 local elections due to ballot shortages, revealing serious negligence in election commission management
Former Chairman Noh Tae-ak wasted over 12 million won in taxpayer money on overseas business trips accompanied by his spouse
The election commission has evaded oversight by using independence as a shield, and trust cannot be restored without comprehensive reform
editorial.bodyTr: The National Election Commission has confirmed that severe chaos occurred at some polling places during the June 3 local elections that led to the ballot shortage crisis. According to voting records documenting the situation on election day, at one polling station in Jamsil 2-dong, Seoul, ballots were distributed without the election supervisor's stamp. At other polling stations, ballots were found scattered on the floor, and residents reported that calls to the election commission went unanswered due to ballot shortages. Even after additional ballots arrived, residents who had received waiting tickets did not return, resulting in lost voting opportunities. During last year's presidential election, there were over 2,000 cases of people signing other voters' names and casting ballots, indicating serious mismanagement across the entire election administration system. There were reasons for this. According to reports, it is a tradition at the election commission that the number of employees on leave actually increases during years when election work becomes busy. This raises questions about whether this organization is qualified to manage fair elections. The problem extends beyond mere negligence. It is becoming increasingly clear that the election commission's leadership has fallen into serious ethical lapses. Former Election Commission Chairman Roh Tae-ak accompanied his spouse on all three overseas business trips during his tenure, including visits to Denmark and Sweden. The election commission paid for business-class airfare exceeding 12 million won and other expenses. Using taxpayer money for overseas visits related to elections that were neither official diplomatic missions nor major state affairs is difficult to defend against criticism of junket travel. The fact that official trip reports did not mention "accompanied by spouse" makes clear that this was outright favoritism. Given the conduct of the agency head, employee misconduct was virtually inevitable. Election commission staff went on business trips to world-renowned vacation destinations like the Maldives and brazenly attached beach photos to reports, claiming they would be used in election promotional materials. They sent employees yearly to tourist destinations like Italy under the guise of capacity building. Internal discipline has deteriorated so severely that even when suspicions arose of "nepotistic hiring" among senior election commission officials, junket travel continued unabated. The election commission, using independence as a shield to avoid external oversight and checks while creating a sanctuary, has reached a point where it cannot recover public trust without comprehensive reform. Transparency in budget execution must be secured, and a monitoring system to prevent hiring irregularities must be introduced. Elections managed by an election commission that has lost public trust risk leaving seeds of doubt about their outcomes.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 공공기관의 도덕적 해이와 특권, 투명성 부족을 강하게 비판하며 감시 체계 강화를 요구하는 진보적 프레이밍으로, 권력 기관의 부실과 기강 해이에 대한 진보진영의 전형적 관점을 드러냄.

editorial.title: The threshold of seniority
editorial.body: Seoul’s transit reform highlights need to redefine aging in a superaged Korea In politics, age thresholds often survive long after the realities that produced them have disappeared. What begins as a practical benchmark can harden into convention and eventually into policy dogma. South Korea’s definition of old age is approaching that stage. The Seoul Metropolitan Council's decision this week to introduce bus fare support for residents aged 70 and over, alongside discussions about raising the free subway ride eligibility age from 65 to 70, is ostensibly a transportation policy. In reality, it is a test case for something much larger. The immediate pressure comes from fiscal reality. Seoul Metro recorded a deficit of 826.8 billion won ($537 million) last year, of which free rides for seniors accounted for 448.8 billion won. Over the past five years, losses linked to the program have exceeded 3 trillion won. The proposed reform seeks to rebalance, rather than simply reduce, support. Under the plan, citizens aged 70 and older would receive subsidized bus rides, helping address a longstanding blind spot for seniors living in neighborhoods without convenient subway access. The city estimates the annual cost at roughly 50 billion to 60 billion won, far below the savings expected from raising the subway eligibility age. Yet the debate extends far beyond transit budgets. The benchmark age of 65 was codified in the Senior Welfare Act in 1981, when life expectancy stood at 66.7 years. Today it has risen to 83.7 years. Koreans aged 65 and older now account for over 20 percent of the population, making the country a “superaged society.” By 2030, that figure is projected to reach 25.5 percent. Social perceptions have changed just as dramatically. Recent polling indicates that nearly six in 10 Koreans support raising the senior age criterion to 70, while surveys show that many citizens now view the onset of old age as beginning around then. For a growing share of people in their late 60s, employment, volunteering and social participation remain routine parts of life. The label “elderly” no longer carries the same practical meaning it did four decades ago. More revealing is that Seoul's proposal has emerged while national reform remains stalled. The need to reconsider the senior age threshold has been discussed since at least 2019, yet successive governments have hesitated to deal with it. Despite years of consultations and expert reviews, little tangible progress has followed. Political caution is understandable given that the issue touches pensions, healthcare, welfare programs and employment policy. But caution has gradually become inertia. In the absence of a national framework, local governments have begun improvising. Daegu has already linked bus subsidies and subway benefits to a phased increase in eligibility ages, with plans to align both systems at 70. Such local experimentation may prove useful, but it cannot substitute for a coherent national strategy. Yet policymakers must not assume that raising the threshold alone solves the problem. South Korea continues to record the highest elderly poverty rate in the OECD. If welfare eligibility ages rise while retirement practices remain largely unchanged, a gap could emerge between the end of stable employment and access to public support. That would merely shift costs from public accounts to household finances. The debate over seniority must be connected to retirement age policies, reemployment opportunities and the design of better welfare programs. It may also require moving beyond universal age-based benefits toward systems that take income and financial need into account. A society that lives longer cannot indefinitely rely on definitions inherited from a very different era. Seoul's transit proposal offers a glimpse of that overdue discussion. The objective is not to spend less on older citizens but to build institutions that reflect longer lives, changing patterns of work and the realities of a superaged country. S. editorial.pullQuote: A society that lives longer cannot indefinitely rely on definitions inherited from a very different era.
summary: Seoul proposes raising senior transit benefits eligibility from 65 to 70, reflecting longer lifespans and changing work patterns.
The 65-year-old threshold, set in 1981 when life expectancy was 67, no longer matches today's 84-year average.
National reform stalled, but local governments are experimenting—though true change requires coordinated policy across pensions and employment.
editorial.stance: Age thresholds must evolve with demographics; raising eligibility requires parallel reforms to retirement and employment policy, not cost-cutting alone.
summaryTr: 서울이 노인 대중교통 혜택 수급 나이를 65세에서 70세로 상향 조정할 것을 제안했으며, 이는 증가한 평균수명과 변화하는 근무 양식을 반영한 것이다.
1981년 기대수명이 67세였을 때 설정된 65세 기준은 현재의 84세 평균 기대수명과 더 이상 맞지 않는다.
국가 차원의 개혁은 교착 상태에 있지만, 지방자치단체들이 실험을 거듭하고 있으며, 진정한 변화를 위해서는 연금과 고용을 아우르는 조율된 정책이 필요하다.
editorial.bodyTr:
서울의 대중교통 개혁이 드러내는 초고령사회 한국의 노년 정의 재정의 필요성

정치에서 연령 기준은 그것을 만들어낸 현실이 사라진 지 오래 후에도 종종 남아있다. 실용적인 지표로 시작된 것이 관례로 굳어지고 결국 정책 교리가 되기도 한다. 한국의 노년 정의가 그 단계에 접근하고 있다. 서울시의회가 이번 주 70세 이상 주민을 대상으로 한 버스 요금 지원을 도입하기로 결정하고 지하철 무임승차 대상 연령을 65세에서 70세로 상향할 것을 논의한 것은 표면상으로는 대중교통 정책이다. 하지만 실제로는 훨씬 더 큰 문제를 시험하는 사례다.

직접적인 압박은 재정 현실에서 비롯된다. 서울메트로는 지난해 8268억 원의 적자를 기록했으며, 이 중 노인 무임승차로 인한 손실이 4488억 원에 달했다. 지난 5년간 이 프로그램과 관련된 손실이 3조 원을 초과했다. 제안된 개혁은 단순히 지원을 줄이는 것이 아니라 재균형을 맞추려는 것이다. 계획에 따르면 70세 이상 시민은 버스 요금 지원을 받게 되어 지하철 접근이 불편한 지역에 사는 노인들의 오랫동안 방치되어온 문제를 해결할 수 있다. 서울시는 연간 비용을 약 500억~600억 원으로 추정하고 있으며, 이는 지하철 대상 연령 상향으로 기대할 수 있는 절감액을 크게 밑돈다. 그러나 이 논쟁은 대중교통 예산을 훨씬 초과한다.

65세라는 기준은 1981년 노인복지법에 규정되었을 때 기대수명이 66.7세였다. 오늘날 기대수명은 83.7세로 상승했다. 65세 이상 한국인이 인구의 20% 이상을 차지하면서 한국은 '초고령사회'가 되었다. 2030년에는 그 비율이 25.5%에 이를 것으로 예상된다. 사회적 인식도 극적으로 변했다. 최근 여론조사에 따르면 약 60%에 가까운 한국인이 노년 기준을 70세로 상향하는 것을 지지하고 있으며, 설문조사들은 많은 시민들이 노년의 시작을 70세 정도로 보는 것으로 나타낸다. 60대 후반인 사람들이 증가하면서 취업, 자원봉사, 사회 참여는 여전히 일상의 일부가 되고 있다. '노인'이라는 꼬리표는 40년 전처럼 실질적인 의미를 더 이상 가지지 않는다.

더욱 주목할 점은 서울의 제안이 나타난 것이 국가 차원의 개혁이 교착 상태에 있는 동안이라는 것이다. 노년 기준을 재검토해야 한다는 필요성은 최소한 2019년부터 논의되어 왔으나, 역대 정부들은 이를 다루기를 망설여왔다. 수년간의 협의와 전문가 검토에도 불구하고 실질적인 진전이 거의 없었다. 연금, 의료, 복지 프로그램, 고용 정책 등 여러 분야를 건드리기 때문에 정치적 신중함은 이해할 수 있다. 하지만 신중함이 점차 답보 상태로 변했다. 국가 차원의 틀이 없는 가운데 지방 정부들이 독자적으로 움직이기 시작했다. 대구는 이미 버스 지원과 지하철 혜택을 단계적 대상 연령 상향과 연결했으며, 두 체계를 모두 70세로 조정할 계획을 갖고 있다. 이러한 지방 실험이 유용할 수 있지만, 일관된 국가 전략을 대체할 수 없다.

그러나 정책입안자들은 기준 상향만으로 문제가 해결된다고 가정해서는 안 된다. 한국은 여전히 OECD 내 최고의 노인 빈곤율을 기록하고 있다. 복지 대상 연령이 상향되면서 정년 관행이 크게 변하지 않는다면, 안정적인 고용의 종료와 공공 지원 접근 사이의 간격이 벌어질 수 있다. 그렇게 되면 공공 재정의 비용이 가계 재정으로만 옮겨갈 것이다. 노년에 대한 논쟁은 정년 정책, 재취업 기회, 더 나은 복지 프로그램 설계와 연결되어야 한다. 또한 보편적 연령 기반 혜택을 넘어 소득과 경제적 필요를 고려하는 체계로 나아가야 할 수도 있다.

더 오래 사는 사회는 매우 다른 시대로부터 물려받은 정의에 무한정 의존할 수 없다. 서울의 대중교통 제안은 그 필요한 논쟁을 일부 보여준다. 목표는 노인들을 위한 지출을 줄이는 것이 아니라 더 긴 삶, 변화하는 일의 패턴, 초고령사회의 현실을 반영하는 제도를 구축하는 것이다.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: The editorial acknowledges fiscal constraints on welfare spending while defending robust support for seniors, proposes evidence-based policy reform over ideology, and avoids attacking either vulnerable groups or business interests.

editorials.title: The threshold of seniority
editorials.body: Seoul’s transit reform highlights need to redefine aging in a superaged Korea In politics, age thresholds often survive long after the realities that produced them have disappeared. What begins as a practical benchmark can harden into convention and eventually into policy dogma. South Korea’s definition of old age is approaching that stage. The Seoul Metropolitan Council's decision this week to introduce bus fare support for residents aged 70 and over, alongside discussions about raising the free subway ride eligibility age from 65 to 70, is ostensibly a transportation policy. In reality, it is a test case for something much larger. The immediate pressure comes from fiscal reality. Seoul Metro recorded a deficit of 826.8 billion won ($537 million) last year, of which free rides for seniors accounted for 448.8 billion won. Over the past five years, losses linked to the program have exceeded 3 trillion won. The proposed reform seeks to rebalance, rather than simply reduce, support. Under the plan, citizens aged 70 and older would receive subsidized bus rides, helping address a longstanding blind spot for seniors living in neighborhoods without convenient subway access. The city estimates the annual cost at roughly 50 billion to 60 billion won, far below the savings expected from raising the subway eligibility age. Yet the debate extends far beyond transit budgets. The benchmark age of 65 was codified in the Senior Welfare Act in 1981, when life expectancy stood at 66.7 years. Today it has risen to 83.7 years. Koreans aged 65 and older now account for over 20 percent of the population, making the country a “superaged society.” By 2030, that figure is projected to reach 25.5 percent. Social perceptions have changed just as dramatically. Recent polling indicates that nearly six in 10 Koreans support raising the senior age criterion to 70, while surveys show that many citizens now view the onset of old age as beginning around then. For a growing share of people in their late 60s, employment, volunteering and social participation remain routine parts of life. The label “elderly” no longer carries the same practical meaning it did four decades ago. More revealing is that Seoul's proposal has emerged while national reform remains stalled. The need to reconsider the senior age threshold has been discussed since at least 2019, yet successive governments have hesitated to deal with it. Despite years of consultations and expert reviews, little tangible progress has followed. Political caution is understandable given that the issue touches pensions, healthcare, welfare programs and employment policy. But caution has gradually become inertia. In the absence of a national framework, local governments have begun improvising. Daegu has already linked bus subsidies and subway benefits to a phased increase in eligibility ages, with plans to align both systems at 70. Such local experimentation may prove useful, but it cannot substitute for a coherent national strategy. Yet policymakers must not assume that raising the threshold alone solves the problem. South Korea continues to record the highest elderly poverty rate in the OECD. If welfare eligibility ages rise while retirement practices remain largely unchanged, a gap could emerge between the end of stable employment and access to public support. That would merely shift costs from public accounts to household finances. The debate over seniority must be connected to retirement age policies, reemployment opportunities and the design of better welfare programs. It may also require moving beyond universal age-based benefits toward systems that take income and financial need into account. A society that lives longer cannot indefinitely rely on definitions inherited from a very different era. Seoul's transit proposal offers a glimpse of that overdue discussion. The objective is not to spend less on older citizens but to build institutions that reflect longer lives, changing patterns of work and the realities of a superaged country. S. editorials.pullQuote: A society that lives longer cannot indefinitely rely on definitions inherited from a very different era.
summary: Seoul proposes raising senior transit benefits eligibility from 65 to 70, reflecting longer lifespans and changing work patterns.
The 65-year-old threshold, set in 1981 when life expectancy was 67, no longer matches today's 84-year average.
National reform stalled, but local governments are experimenting—though true change requires coordinated policy across pensions and employment.
editorials.stance: Age thresholds must evolve with demographics; raising eligibility requires parallel reforms to retirement and employment policy, not cost-cutting alone.

editorial.title: [사설] 정부 “보완수사권 폐지”, 국회서 최적 방안 찾아야
editorial.body: 김민석 국무총리가 25일 “검찰 보완수사권 폐지를 정부의 기본 입장으로 최종 정리했다”며 “국회의 자유로운 논의를 위해 별도의 정부안은 제출하지 않기로 했다”고 브리핑을 통해 밝혔다. 당정은 그동안 ‘수사-기소 분리’라는 검찰개혁의 기본 방향에 합의하고도 검찰의 보완수
editorial.pullQuote: 검찰 보완수사권 폐지를 정부의 기본 입장으로 최종 정리했다
summary: 정부, 검찰 보완수사권 폐지를 최종 입장으로 결정했다.
국회의 자유로운 논의를 위해 별도 정부안 제출 안 하기로.
수사-기소 분리 원칙에 따른 검찰개혁 추진 중.
editorial.stance: 검찰 개혁의 기본 방향에 합의하되 최적 방안은 국회에서 결정해야 한다는 입장
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: Government Calls for Abolishing Supplementary Investigation Authority; Parliament Must Find Best Solution
summaryTr: The government has made a final decision to abolish the prosecution's supplementary investigative powers.
The government decided not to submit a separate proposal to allow for free discussion in the National Assembly.
The prosecution reform is being pursued in accordance with the principle of separating investigation and indictment.
editorial.bodyTr: Prime Minister Kim Min-seok announced on the 25th through a briefing that "the abolition of the prosecution's supplementary investigation authority has been finalized as the government's basic position" and that "we have decided not to submit a separate government proposal to allow for free discussion in the National Assembly." The ruling party and government had previously agreed on the basic direction of prosecution reform through "separation of investigation and prosecution," but the supplementary investigation authority of the prosecution remains a pending issue.

summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung reaffirms commitment to pursuing 'selective recruitment system'.
Defense Ministry announces concrete implementation plan.
Military structural reform driven by population decline and AI ubiquity.
editorials.bodyTr: President Lee Jae-myung reiterated his commitment to "selective recruitment," his longstanding position to minimize conscription and make the military a career choice through voluntary enlistment. In response, the Defense Ministry unveiled concrete implementation plans. With the population decline sharply reducing available manpower resources and the widespread use of unmanned robots and artificial intelligence becoming commonplace, the military structure is undergoing significant transformation.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Government Must Heed Public Voices on Using Semiconductor Windfall Profits
summaryTr: Calls are growing to use semiconductor windfall profits as funding for designing the nation's future.
The debate is expanding beyond Samsung Electronics' bonus controversy to broader social discussion on how to recoup windfall profits.
Beyond corporate compensation, there is a need to gather civil society opinions on how to wisely use windfall profits.
editorials.bodyTr: Growing voices are calling for excess profits generated by the semiconductor boom to be used as seed money for designing the nation's future. The controversy sparked by Samsung Electronics' massive performance bonuses has evolved beyond internal corporate compensation, developing into a broader social debate about how to recover excess profits and where it would be wisest to invest them.

editorials.body: [사설] 원·달러 환율 1550원 눈앞…무엇이든 진정 조치 나와야, 오피니언
editorials.pullQuote: 원·달러 환율 1550원 눈앞…무엇이든 진정 조치 나와야
summary: 원·달러 환율이 1550원대에 육박하고 있다.
정부는 더 이상 미루지 말고 즉각적인 대응책을 마련해야 한다.
환율 급등으로 경제 전반의 불안정성이 커지고 있다.
editorials.stance: 환율 급등 위기에 대한 정부의 적극적이고 즉각적인 개입이 필수적이다.
editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Won-Dollar Rate Nearing 1,550 Won... Real Stabilization Measures Needed Now
summaryTr: The won-dollar exchange rate is approaching the 1,550 won level.
The government must devise immediate measures without further delay.
Economic instability is growing across the board due to the sharp rise in exchange rates.
